Goan people in my neighborhood are good, but non goans in my neighborhood are jealous and annoying (karnataka people). But we love the locals. Fight with us because when it rains some water falls on their house etc small bullshit.

responses.electricityComment: no

responses.businessComment: it is good nothing to change
responses.loveText: wonderful, quiet neighborhood that is almost perfect—if only the local authorities would do their job and keep the infrastructure up to par.
responses.commentsText: gutters and 24 hours water and underground electricity was promised still waiting for it and if they give 24 hours water please give meter at the same time otherwise people will misuse it roads management should be done sweeper should be appointed

responses.roadComment: Even the most prominent road, like MG Road is full of bumps and pots.

responses.loveText: It’s close to the beach, great location.

responses.roadComment: Patchwork and repair is done that lasts from monsoon to monsoon. Once a year sewage pipes are clean and garbage piled on the edge, never again to be removed. Monsoon washes it back into the drains - corruption issue.
responses.loveText: No sound pollution, less air pollution due to greenery, large open space for me to have a kitchen garden, family and support system in the area, only 1 entry gate so safe for kids to play.
responses.commentsText: If only we as human beings and citizens could change and stop failing each other. The government cannot do everything for us. We are the most unhygienic people and we make our areas like open bathroom spitoons. Traffic jams also due to green and a lack of pride in their jobs.

responses.electricityComment: Decent but gets disrupted during rainy days
responses.roadComment: Absolute worst, half dug up roads, loose gravel all around , dust
responses.safetyComment: Lot secure than most neighbourhoods
responses.loveText: Greenary
responses.commentsText: Political hoardings everywhere, even the bus stops have politician face on it and on parks every bench is carved with politician name who is a local mla, I have raised rti but no response

responses.electricityComment: Occasionally powercuts happen, especially during monsoon
responses.roadComment: The quality of the roads is okay but there is too much traffic everywhere, constant honking and rule breaking is very common here. There is construction going on everywhere which has ruined the air quality and has increased the traffic severally
responses.safetyComment: There are several areas in the neighborhood where class 1 drugs such as weed maybe sold, the authorities already know about it but it still is rampant. Most of these people are tippers and work for the police.
responses.businessComment: Homestly, more cleanliness and accesible roads would be amazing, there are normal street vendors and vegetable vendors that take 40% of the road in some areas, a fixed area for this would be better
responses.loveText: I have grown up in this neighborhood growing up it was very united community, people from all walks of life and religion would coexist. But lately there have been instances where communal politics has been seen and because of that certian incidents have also happened which have been somehow stopped by the locals to not make it a huge deal.
responses.commentsText: The water quality we get is quite poor, for eg my Buidlung has BMC supply, and a Borewell supply which we have created. For the past 6 months we are getting water (BMC) that is smelly, and seems like sewage water, on days where we do get it, it is very minimal. If we rely on borewell we have been getting less and less because someone in the area has started a water tanmer business and is oulling from the groundwater supply which is lowering the water level for everyone.

responses.roadComment: Roads are getting damaged by construction of houses , but after construction both owner nor the government fixes the road may after few years they deside to fill the pothole but still will be like a patch not smooth as before
responses.businessComment: Parking spaces and good footpath without vehicle and blocked by random stuffs
responses.loveText: It's very central location to Bangalore but still not at all crowded it's like peace but as central you can get before evening gets crowded

responses.electricityComment: Supply is good, throughout the year. However, the electrical poles are not maintained well. It is quite risky at some places.
responses.roadComment: They build concrete roads every few years, for drainage, and they say, they forgot install pipes and they build again, but the build quality is bad.
responses.commentsText: There is no drainage system in my neighbourhood yet. All my lifetime, we still have open gutters. However recently they installed drainage system pipes inside the roads, but the pipes they installed are so low quality, and the size of the pipe is just 6 inches. I wonder, how is that gonna hold up.

responses.loveText: nothing

responses.electricityComment: supply is good but bills are too too too too high(adani btw)
responses.roadComment: potholes, potholes & potholes
responses.safetyComment: too many wires, no management

responses.electricityComment: Atleast we get subsidised electricity
responses.loveText: Well atleast its accessible to major hubs in Bangalore easily, metro, buses and other public transports are easily accessible

responses.electricityComment: Severe voltage fluctuations and inconsistent power supply

responses.safetyComment: Police is not to help us but to exploit commoners

responses.loveText: The neighborhood had good green space at one point of time but now it's reducing drastically. That's why planning to change my neighbour hood

responses.businessComment: Parking congestion can be improved ,footpaths can be build ,proper parking spaces have to be available
responses.loveText: Less congestion and large open places ( private and public offices ) keep the area relatively cool due to trees and air flow and ventilation is not a problem but things are beginning to change.
responses.commentsText: Overall there are systems that are in place but they donot work everytime and often donot coearly address the problem .

responses.electricityComment: No power cut and power backup available

responses.safetyComment: I live in a gated society and most area around me is that but, there are unsafe spaces in the corners or slums in the area
responses.businessComment: Parking infrastructure and no parking on road implementation
responses.loveText: Its safer and cleaner than surrounding area. Market is close by and work is approachable. Otherwise i seldom leave house
responses.commentsText: Too costly for the services being offered and congestion in market is a problem. And its extremely unsafe and hazardous for pedestrians

responses.roadComment: No spaces for walking, cycling, access to market other parts of area bottlenecked because of overflowing unclean drain, MCD garbage dumps overflowing with area around unclean, full with stray dogs, animals!
responses.loveText: Community, still keeping what somewhat village community in a urban/semi urban town-ish village midst in middle of city!

responses.electricityComment: Nowadays a lot of random powercuts are observed
responses.roadComment: It's not worth calling them roads especially when you've lived abroad where roads are designed... Let's call these alleyways always under construction and still have potholes and uneven levelling
responses.loveText: Compared to most countries abroad and most other parts of India, I feel extremely safe in my neighborhood and largely the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, even if my wife were to travel late in the night alone, touch wood safety hasn't been a big concern so far.
responses.commentsText: It's also been BJP and RSS 'Balekilla' with decades of their stronghold  and cultural influence.

responses.electricityComment: The area where I live doesn't still has government connection reached so a post property owner of this area installed a transformer lf his own money and charges extra too extra for electricity connection about 50000 for it's availability at your house followed up by bills later.
responses.roadComment: It's not even built it's a raw (kacchi road here in my society) It's a undeveloped society but roads are needed. We personally had to made dumb some damur material to make this raw road lil fine as in rainy days it's because bizzare.
responses.safetyComment: Lack of safety regular theft take place here and not safe to go out even cases of kidnapping and murders are pretty often nearby
responses.loveText: It's not too populated yet so i get some peace here although it's not gonna last more than 5 years many apartments are being built so it's gonna be overpopulated soon 🤧🤧🥺
responses.commentsText: Some parks and even local shops are lacking here I've to go pretty far to buy stationary amd other stuff there's no development here no safety no roads and electricity is cutted off every night for too long man we need to sleep after long working days and lack of safety people lacking civic sense eve teasing is too much commenting is too much I've been inappropriately touched twice in public places here been bad  commented tons of times .

responses.loveText: That we don't care about educating ourselves citizen participation, and we love to compain and blame than take action
responses.commentsText: A strong community that can have deep roots in local governing body is missing since the last 20 years in 560075

responses.electricityComment: Very frequent power cuts some times
responses.roadComment: Illegal encroachment in front of my society itself, the person is not allowing to build roads
responses.commentsText: Please help in building proper roads

responses.electricityComment: Ridiculously expensive. Senseless usage policy. Maharashtra (MSEDCL) probably is the most expensive DISCOM in the country for residential electricity. You pay almost double with every slab in and around KDMC(my municipality). And the charges for usage from 0-100 would directly double if you used 101 units on a certain month. Same with 100-300, 300-500 and 500+. Charges don't get separated slab-wise!
responses.roadComment: I know roads in entire Maharashtra are pretty bad.. But I don't know what it is about Mumbai, Thane, Kalyan, Dombivali, Ulhasnagar. Our roads are really badly planned and horribly made. The upkeep is an excuse for contractors to make money. Speed breakers hit cars with very high ground clearence. Which leads to entire city buying taller and bigger cars.
responses.safetyComment: There are entitled teenager of a certain political and community classes which behave like the own the streets, drive horribly, Dark out the glasses. Cops do nothing about them.
responses.businessComment: Road, Walkability, Garbage. City itself is a hazard for the general populous.
responses.loveText: My closer neighbourhood has good people, well educated and roads have been recently concretised.
responses.commentsText: Make solar net metering easier in Maharashtra! Why is this not a priority?

responses.electricityComment: There are power cuts for multiple hours during peak summer. This happens about 5-6 times every month.
responses.roadComment: They do clean and maintain the roads well, but the footpaths aren't as well maintained.
responses.safetyComment: Kamothe is a very calm town overall, I've never faced or heard of anyone facing any safety issue, but streetlights do need a bit of improvement.
responses.loveText: People are generally nice, there's good connectivity to Mumbai, Pune and Thane. Good restaurants and snack corners, multiple gyms, pretty close to NMI airport, multiple hospitals and banks close by, and comparatively affordable housing than the rest of Navi Mumbai
responses.commentsText: They NEED TO have better cleanliness and larger public parks, the current ones are crowded, and better management of traffic is required too.

responses.electricityComment: These days power cuts have become frequent. Another problem is MSEB officers whom we called for damaged meter asked us to come there physically to file complain but upon going there, perhaps looking at my privileged face, they conveyed that I didn't have to come.
responses.roadComment: There are cement roads everywhere but strips have been excavated for water pipeline have not been repaired.
responses.safetyComment: People drive very fast in the residential place. Some follow girls on bikes, some on feet. Girls playing badminton or merely strolling out of their houses gets honked at or they maybe weird noises. Some affray occurs once a while. Drunkard pass out on the streets or worse in the gate.
responses.loveText: Market places are very close.
responses.commentsText: Our corporator who recently hit elected, he has worked very well for us in the past, on the election day there was an affray on the polling station as people from rural side came to vote in corporation election. It was alleged that around 700 people were brought from rural side. Also whole election was speculated to be rigged as 45% of population voted i.e. 5.5 lakhs but more than 2 lakh people voted after 17:00 and didn't see voting going on after time.

responses.electricityComment: It’s more of a rural township in wester ghats. So there’d be constant power cuts. Situation is worse as we move interior
responses.loveText: Comparatively clean air and less pollution

responses.electricityComment: There are frequent power cuts, especially in summers, in mae of - preparing for monsoons - but the first rains and boom, no power! What preparation!
responses.roadComment: There is no road right now. Honestly this is. Bad time to respond to this survey for Nashik, because the city is dig up in "preparation of the Kumbh". But my rating is for my area even otherwise.
responses.safetyComment: I stay in a neighbourhood with no development around. Covered in bushes. Feels like anything could be hiding there, with increase in leopard sightings around, it's kinda scary. Not so scary to drive, but walk I won't.
responses.businessComment: Better road with footpath, and parking. Proper planning.
responses.loveText: It's quiet and less traffic. So long!
responses.commentsText: No

responses.loveText: Clean, good garbage collection

responses.loveText: Since my area is on the outskirts of the city, the light pollution here is lesser than that in the main city so I am able to look at stars sometimes.
responses.commentsText: The levels of pollution here is horrible mostly air pollution (dust) (avg AQI 120) that is caused by the going on metro construction.

responses.loveText: Gated community, kids can play outside without supervision.

responses.roadComment: In telangana except hyderabad all the roads are great. village roads, town roads are very well maintained but when it comes to hyderabad may be due to kichidi of Bureaucracy, mayor, and govt lot many decision takers involved the problem exists.
responses.loveText: People, Reasons is people have the guts to go live on media and scold CM. Telangana culture have the spirit of questioning the authorities that what evolved as telangana movement.
responses.commentsText: In telangana the basic (includes villages, towns, cities) good roads, 24hr electricity, water connection to all house holds and every district has a Reservoir for agriculture. Mission bhagiratha - every home gets water connectioin for free sucessful. Mission kakatiya - i dont know what happened. mission haritha haram - successful hope govt plan fro 2.0, kg to pg flop. If possible i request to make a video on TIMS telangana.

responses.electricityComment: Was a lil inconsistent during the stormy days this year thats the only thing stoping it from five stars
responses.roadComment: They were great then dug up for new sewage pipes
responses.safetyComment: Noida is generally safe till you go to unplanned parts
responses.loveText: Its much more structured and planned with wide enough roads green spaces and public infrastructure
responses.commentsText: For all the great things in noida generally for a public quite comfy there are few very distrubing serial killer american psyco crime cases that we never imagined happens here pop up time to time , yeah you can look that up usually around unplanned parts like the parts that predate the planned noida for example mithari

responses.electricityComment: Electricity Supply is OK
responses.roadComment: Main Roads are okish, but the branch roads and inside colonies is horrible or non existent.
responses.safetyComment: I am in Chambal so what to expect
responses.loveText: Very Little, people get work done through connections, so the govt machinery is just for namesake.
responses.commentsText: The city we are in is a failed smart city project.

responses.roadComment: no public dustbins so people throw trash on road
responses.loveText: safety

responses.roadComment: congestion Is horrible
responses.loveText: Peaceful, less noisy

responses.electricityComment: Rate cuts happens every month for maintenance, but increases during monsoon specially.
responses.roadComment: Lot of construction around my area, with lot of trucks moving around. Also, trucks moving at night for dumping construction waste in nearby river.

responses.electricityComment: When it gets windy or there is cloud coverage and its rainy the shut down the electricity, otherwise its okay. Due to AAP government scheme Of free 300 units it has become affordable for many people but due to frequent seasonal electricity cutoff many people have diesel generators at home due to which fuel costs add up
responses.roadComment: Road quality is good in my town but drainage is very bad some areas clog up water with very few mm of rainfall. For example the road in front of the grain mandi of our town is clogged up with water every time it rains but authorities refuse to higher its elevation due to the fear of mandi being flooded. They are idiots who refuse to construct a drainage system.
responses.safetyComment: There is drug trafficking in some areas of the town but most of the drug related activities occurs mostly out of town mostly in villages. Marginalised sections of the society such as Dalits and poor farming communities are the most devastated by this drug trade.
responses.businessComment: Like i said previously an shade in summer heat for the small market of our town will greatly increase customers in the market And in my business, which is of selling clothes.
responses.loveText: this town is its surrounded by farms and greenery as far as the eye can see and there is a lovely Sutlej river 10 km from the town
responses.commentsText: I live in the min market of our town (building has three floors 1st and the ground floor is my shop and the topmost floor is my home). Being in the market you can guess the community which lives near the market and works in the market. Most of the people in the community outwardly don’t care about caste and sit and eat with everyone but when they talk between their community they throw every kind of casteist remark imaginable about everyone. This type of ideas they spread to their kids who also develop preconceived notion about types of people they belong to insted of seeing whole of humanity as their tribe.

responses.electricityComment: Sometimes due to high demand a small fire breaks out in the wires
responses.roadComment: Lots of potholes and some construction material clogging the entire street
responses.safetyComment: It is safe in terms of people but lots of stray dogs even though they are usually calm
responses.loveText: Quiet, friendly people and proximity to metro station
responses.commentsText: I think if cleanliness was maintained and some more trees were planted + honking was limited it would be a perfect place to live

responses.electricityComment: Its good as in we get reliable electricity supply. But like adoption of new technologies is not happening or facilities for people who want to have solar panels installed on roof or terrace and EV infrastructure is just pathetic and this is in Metro city of Kolkata.
responses.roadComment: There's no proper drainage. Also although our neighbourhood is connected and part of the famous Barrackpore Trunk Road but waterlogging is a very big problem and not proper lane and zebra crossing markings and no safe space for walking of pedestrians and potholes and uneven roads are a reality everywhere.
responses.safetyComment: See I didn't want to be political but the vidhan sabha constituency I live - Madan Mitra from TMC (Kamarhati) won and it's also a muslim majority constituency. But other constituencies around have been won by BJP. Now I am kinda worried that during festivals I feel like jhamela na ho. Already we had some small incidents where Blinkit delivery boys who happened to be muslim were forcefully applied upon Saffron coloured Abir powder the day BJP won in West Bengal elections just cause they were celebrating.
responses.businessComment: I guess no intimidation from the authorities and more deregulation and encouragement of in general and entrepreneurial temperament among the people as we have seen 34 years of left rule. I feel people think all profits are dirty and kind of seeing entrepreneurship with contempt and discourage the natural scaling of a business from small to big ethically.
responses.loveText: Harmony and Syncreticism and helpfulness within misery
responses.commentsText: Bengal needs entrepreneurship and huge Infrastructure investments and Jobs and also we need to fix our schools we have an absolutely ruined education sector and job sector. People have been fleeing from the state. Don't want cultural clashes just development. Also we want the state to take step back and not interfere and have meritocratic appointments and not politically motivated. We need free and fair and transparent execution in government schemes and law and order.

responses.electricityComment: Very unreliable and too much power cuts
responses.roadComment: Broken and dusty. Very narrow.
responses.safetyComment: Police will do all the things which they are suppose to stop
responses.businessComment: Development of market. Proper organization, garbage management, public toilets should be there. During monsoon, water gets logged right in front of the shop and it creates a hurdle for customers to enter the shop, and it takes water weeks to proper drain.
responses.loveText: Nothing. We live here cause it’s close to the capital.
responses.commentsText: MLA/MP only takes care of their own locality, from where he gets the maximum votes. The area nearby his house is lush green with good roads, drainage system, garbage management, proper 24 x 7 electricity, no stay animals. But when it comes to other areas, condition is very bad.

responses.roadComment: It's the worst than ever, too many potholes. It's a national highway but it's still a two lane so anyone can imagine the congestion and traffic.
responses.safetyComment: Again all the problems except of water logging ciz of the rugged terrain of this area. Biggest civic hazard would be the burning of plastics, tires and other materials by shop owners or even common people out in the open. No one collects any garbage, and this will be the biggest hazard for the locals! Park and footpaths don't even exist. Dust and other pollutants are a daily staple of the residents here. 🙂
responses.businessComment: This is a bit odd that i selected all the options which have been provided here!! One can imagine the situation here.
responses.loveText: I like the different types of birds i see here despite being a noisy neighbourhood (horns, vehicles, noisy temple bhajans, machines etc). Will always get surprised by seeing new type of birds on a random evening in my backyard. Apart from that, it maybe not unique to this place but people here are helpful, if the accidents happen they'll surely help them to carry them to local hospitals and all.
responses.commentsText: Garbage not being collected by the concerned authorities is so concerning! Even when a person wants to keep his locality clean, how would he able to do it if there isn't any system for it! Not just that, govt should be transparent about what do they do with the garbage, do they actually segregate it and make good use of it, or do they just dump it elsewhere. In our district headquarter, sewage water is directly being dumped to the neighbouring river, woww!!  Sewage plant doesn't even exist!!

responses.electricityComment: We have good backup in our housing but there is often electricity blackout in our locality.
responses.roadComment: Tbh it could be worse.
responses.businessComment: We need better roads.
responses.loveText: Being a small neighbourhood it is quite peaceful.

responses.roadComment: Everything is broken since over a year. Unwalkable roads. They’re waiting for election to construct- next year
responses.loveText: People are nice, small town.
